50ddcac61004de2b.about:
Researcher @ Thinking Machines Lab, building things one auto-accept-edit-on at a time .

Past:
- Mistral: laid the foundation of full audio stack, core contributor to data pipeline, tokenizer, pretraining, post-training, and audio-out inference. also worked on small LLMs. all results open-sourced with report.
- Nvidia: research intern, multiple publications in audio foundation models.
- FAIR: research intern, published in (1) unsupervised learning methods for speech; (2) pretraining framework for audio generation. The technique was later productized for Meta's multimodal generative models.
- MIT: doctoral research in speech representation learning and generative models.

See personal webpage for more info: https://alexander-h-liu.github.io/

614e3db3628f730f.headline: CEO @ ARI Health | Obstetric Care Application Development | Computational Biology & AI Research Scientist | NVIDIA Inception Program & Google Deepmind Fellow
614e3db3628f730f.about:
Alexis is a scientist turned operator building healthcare AI and care delivery infrastructure for obstetric care. As Founder and CEO of ARI Health, she leads the end-to-end build of MaternID, connecting longitudinal clinical validation and causal modeling research with FDA-aligned product execution, provider and payer partnerships, and actuarial unit economic design for a scalable, risk-bearing maternity care model. Her long-term vision is to establish a vertically integrated, closed-loop obstetric care delivery and decision support system, built to assume total clinical and financial accountability for pregnancy outcomes.



Underpinning this vision is a technical foundation utilizing the following systems:

Product Experience: 
• Computer Vision: Real-time multimodal sensor fusion, high-fidelity feature extraction pipelines, and semantic segmentation. 
• Stochastic Systems: Non-linear state estimation (EKF/UKF) and Lie Algebra manifold optimization for predictive modeling.
• Neural Architectures: Custom transformer-based inference engines and Multimodal RAG architecture.
• Controls: Application of stochastic control theory to complex multimodal signal processing stacks.

Operating systems: 
• Linux (Debian/Ubuntu Server, Custom Kernels)
• Darwin (macOS Kernel / Apple Silicon environment)
• Real-Time Linux (PREEMPT_RT)

Languages & Protocols: 
• Python (JAX/Flax), C++ (17/20), C, CUDA C, ARM64 Assembly, Swift, Bash 
• CMake, Bazel, Prometheus, OpenTelemetry 
• SQL, gRPC, Protocol Buffers 
• HL7 FHIR (R4), DICOM (DCMTK/pydicom)

Platforms:
• JAX, PyTorch/TensorFlow, NVIDIA TensorRT, OpenCV, Apple Metal Performance Shaders
• Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE), Google Distributed Cloud, Terraform (IaC)
• Google Confidential VMs, Microsoft Entra ID 

Design and Implementation: 
• Multimodal Inference Engine - Currently engineering a bespoke spatiotemporal reasoning module and low-latency decision engine, optimizing matrix operations for complex state estimation and high-throughput edge deployment integrating secure HL7/DICOM ingestion pipelines.
614e3db3628f730f.top_skills: Research and Development (R&D) • Clinical Research • Artificial Intelligence (AI) •  Healthcare Strategy and Innovation • Biotechnology

89e667b732a13ad0.headline: Member of Technical Staff at TML
89e667b732a13ad0.about:
Andrea is a Member of Technical Staff at Thinking Machines Lab. Previously, he was a Research Scientist at Meta’s Fundamental AI Research (FAIR), where he focused on multimodal language models and LLM-based agents capable of navigating user interfaces to accomplish user goals.

Andrea earned his doctorate in Electronic & Computer Engineering from The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (HKUST). He was honored with the Outstanding Paper Award at ACL 2019 and received the Best Paper Award at the NeurIPS 2019 ConvAI Workshop.

His research has been featured in prominent outlets such as MIT Technology Review and VentureBeat, highlighting its broader impact on the AI and technology landscape.

Beyond his research contributions, Andrea is actively involved in the academic community. He has served as a program committee member and reviewer for leading machine learning and natural language processing conferences, including ACL, EMNLP, NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, and AAAI. He has also contributed as a reviewer to journals such as the Journal of Natural Language Engineering and Computer Speech & Language. In addition, Andrea co-presented a tutorial on Deeper Conversational AI at NeurIPS 2020.

a27394119672249c.headline: Creative Direction | Venture Building | Brand Building | Production Design | Landscape Architecture
a27394119672249c.about:
Camille Cervantes is both a design-thinker and a systems-thinker, allowing her to be both creative in problem solving, and fast in learning and adapting. Even as she found her way into the tech industry, Cam is also a licensed Landscape Architect, a consulting creative director, and freelances as a film production designer. 

She was the lead for Creatives and Performance Marketing for the e-grocery platform Rappit (formerly PureGo), a joint-venture between 917Ventures and Puregold, having a pivotal role in the company's rebrand and its marketing campaigns. Afterwards, she was absorbed into Globe's 917Ventures' Ideation and Acceleration Group, working across different industries and verticals, focusing on growth with data-driven, tech-first innovations.

She was also the Lead Graphic Designer, Set Designer, and Set Decorator for the first season of the US TV Series, "Almost Paradise" by Electric Entertainment and ABS-CBN International, under the production design team of Bigbulb Design Lab, Inc. A Creative Director for Bigbulb's Branding Department, she works with various clients for ideations, activations, film, TVCs, and product development (Drag Den PH, Ben & Ben, discovery+, HBO GO, among others).

As a visual artist that primarily works with acrylic, Cervantes similarly explores the use of pen/ink, digital painting, spray paint, and photo manipulation in her works. In July 2019, her works were under the "DIOS (Among Us)" group exhibit held at the Arbilo Galerie (Paris, France). She was also a finalist in the 2nd season of Art Battle Manila in FringeMNL 2017, and a featured mural artist for the city-wide Makati Makisalo festival in FringeMNL 2018.

a1c58c18a480ee6c.headline: Curious Typist
a1c58c18a480ee6c.about:
I check LinkedIn messages infrequently, perhaps monthly. If you can, reach out via email or phone instead of via InMail - both remain unchanged for over a decade.

I'm not open to new positions right now, and generally pass on paid consultations, focus groups, financial services, and recruiting pitches. I keep my LinkedIn connections limited to folks I've worked with or know personally, and only make referrals when I can genuinely vouch for someone's work.

That said I'm always excited to chat about infra, datacenters, or startups in general - and if you're around SF, I'm typically up for a quick coffee constitutional around the city!
education_lines: University of North Carolina at Charlotte

b97d037e10396f51.headline: AI CTO & Founder | MY project LNN+LLM+QML=AGI | Quantum, Neuro-symbolic AI | Liquid Neural Networks | RWKV | Mamba(SSM)  | Pharmacy AI, Minerals AI | Full‑Stack AI (LLMs, Agents, Fine‑Tuning, RAG, Vision) | Q-Day Q-ark
b97d037e10396f51.about:
Building the "Asymmetric Response" in AI: Efficiency over Brute Force.

While the world burns billions on GPU clusters to scale Transformers, I focus on the next generation of AI architectures: Mamba (SSM), Quantum-Inspired Tensor Networks, RWKV, and Liquid Neural Networks (LNN). My goal is not just to wrap GPT APIs, but to build sovereign, energy-efficient, and mathematically robust AGI systems.

My philosophy is Neuro-Symbolic AGI:
AGI = LNN (Logic/Physics/Causality) + LLM (World Knowledge) + QML (Quantum Speed)
I am a Product-Driven CTO with 12+ years of P&L ownership, turning complex DeepTech concepts into profitable vertical platforms.

My "Secret Arsenal" & Projects:
🚀 Muhaddis AI (Islamic Intelligence):
Tech: Utilizing Mamba/SSM architectures for infinite context processing without the quadratic cost of Transformers.
Mission: Precise Sanad/Matn analysis and source tracing where "hallucinations" are not an option.

🧬 Pharmacy AI & Shafa AI (MedTech):
Tech: Neuro-symbolic engines combined with Knowledge Graphs.
Mission: Clinical decision support where logic 

(System 2) must override probability (System 1).
mining Minerals AI (Industrial):
Tech: Liquid Neural Networks (LNN) and Quantum-Inspired algorithms.

Mission: Adapting to chaos. Managing exploration risks and industrial optimization in dynamic, real-world physical environments.
Core Competencies:
Post-Transformer Architectures: State Space Models (Mamba), Linear Attention (RWKV), ODE-based Networks (LNN).

Sovereign AI: Edge computing, offline-first models, and data privacy.
Full-Stack AI: From training 700B+ models to multi-agent orchestration and RAG.
I don't just predict the future of AI. I engineer it.
b97d037e10396f51.top_skills: Искусственный интеллект • Машинное обучение • Liquid Neural Networks • Quantum Machine Learning • State Space Models

6a8c2d63ea3ecacb.headline: Thinking Machines Lab | Ex Apple Foundation Models, Facebook AI Research | LTI @ CMU
6a8c2d63ea3ecacb.about:
I'm a Member of Technical Staff at Thinking Machines Lab.

Previously, Machine Learning Researcher in the Foundation Models team at Apple, the core team building Large Language Models that power Apple Intelligence and other AI products across the company. I lead several efforts in pretraining, post-training, and evaluation of LLMs focused on model training recipes, data research, and multilinguality.

I have a decade of AI research experience. Previously, I was a Machine Learning Researcher at Apple Foundation Models, leading several efforts in pretraining (data quality, multilinguality) and evaluation of LLMs. Before that, I was a research engineer at Facebook AI Research (Meta AI/FAIR), working on AI for Creativity, and previously on Neural Machine Translation to power translations on Facebook and Instagram.

In the past, I’ve worked as a software engineer at LinkedIn, and as an intern at Facebook AI, Microsoft Research, and Amazon.

Check out my website! https://dpacgopinath.github.io
6a8c2d63ea3ecacb.location: New York, New York, United States

b413a2b1b219f1a7.headline: MTS, Thinking Machines
b413a2b1b219f1a7.about:
I am a software engineer, researcher, and designer. I hope to empower individuals to cultivate passion, insight, and joy; and to help others laugh and smile more.

My open-source software has reached millions of people, spanning work in distributed systems, programming languages, computer graphics, and interaction design. I enjoy working in small teams, sharing fresh engineering and design perspectives.

How I conceptualize my work: Computers influence our daily lives, augment how people think, and shape how we perceive each other and our visions for the world. They're pretty cool! And I think technology is ultimately in service to the wonderful ideas and places; beliefs, aspirations, and dreams of all the people who rely on it.

I have a math degree and a CS graduate degree from Harvard. Not looking for jobs right now.
b413a2b1b219f1a7.location: New York, New York, United States

ee21c33c7a3a4ff1.headline: Tech for Equitable Cities & Health Innovation | Special Ops @ MedGrocer | Duke Scholar & ASEAN Urbanist
ee21c33c7a3a4ff1.about:
Healthcare for All and Cities for People

I am a designer at heart and find joy in reconfiguring systems so that wellbeing is built into the way we live. As a Special Ops Analyst on MedGrocer’s Population Health team, I lead innovation and capacity-building initiatives that strengthen healthcare for over 20,000 patients, testing first-of-kind models that inform the future of healthcare delivery in the country. 

At Duke University and Duke Kunshan University, I specialized in computational research across labs in U.S. and China that aim to reduce inequality in the Global South. These projects spanned co-developing antibiotic alternatives to combat drug-resistant infections, examining the effects of lesser studied product additives on coastal populations, and evaluating the impact of digital health tools. 

Beyond the clinical, I see wellbeing as a relational practice that emerges in the co-creation of space and culture. At Branch Out for Justice, I interviewed feminist leaders to document movement-building across Latin America. As the Lead of Young Urbanists Philippines, I implement platforms and programming that empower youth to advocate for inclusive planning education and youth-led tactical interventions within their cities. 

Across organizational, biological, and urban systems, I see capacity-building—and by extension, self-making—as the art of understanding disparate parts and rewiring them into interconnected strengths, by design.
ee21c33c7a3a4ff1.location: Quezon City, National Capital Region, Philippines

acdeb30c3e0a26ed.headline: Bioinformatician|NGS and RNA-seq Analysis|Python,R,Bash,Linux|Genomics and computational Biology
acdeb30c3e0a26ed.about:
I am a motivated Bioinformatics student with a strong academic foundation in genetics, molecular biology, and computational analysis. My primary interest lies in leveraging technology and data-driven approaches to understand complex biological systems and solve real-world scientific problems.
Through academic research, presentations, and project-based learning, I have developed skills in scientific writing, data interpretation, critical analysis, and the application of bioinformatics tools. I am particularly interested in areas such as genetic algorithms, epigenetics, molecular data analysis, and computational biology workflows.
I am committed to continuous learning, professional growth, and contributing to innovative research in life sciences. I aspire to work in environments where I can apply analytical thinking, technical skills, and scientific knowledge to advance research and healthcare solutions.

5c4a5bcaee091e1e.headline: Founding Head of Operations at Thinking Machines Lab
5c4a5bcaee091e1e.about: Jonathan Lachman is the founding head of operations at Thinking Machines Lab, a frontier AI research and product company that is working to make AI broadly useful and understandable through solid technical foundations, open science, and practical applications. Previously, Jonathan was the head of special projects at OpenAI, where he supported a variety of company-wide strategic initiatives and served on OpenAI’s senior leadership team. Prior to joining OpenAI, Jonathan was on the management teams of Blend (NYSE:BLND) and Leap Motion. From 2014-2017, Jonathan was the senior Obama White House budget official for defense, intelligence, foreign policy, and veterans programs, and represented the Office of Management and Budget on the National Security Council's Deputies Committee. Prior to joining the White House, he served as chief of staff to Deputy Secretaries of Defense Christine Fox and Bob Work, and was a longstanding senior advisor to the late Secretary of Defense Ash Carter. For his contributions to national security, Jonathan received the Defense Department's Distinguished Public Service Award. Jonathan served on the Biden-Harris Transition Team and is a past term member of the Council on Foreign Relations. A graduate of Princeton and Harvard Kennedy School, he lives in San Francisco.

8074c3b108112ffb.headline: Global Tech Executive & AI Advisor and Investor (ex Apple WW Leadership / ex Unicorn Founder)
8074c3b108112ffb.about:
A curious, creative, and confident global leader with 30 years of experience across tech companies, small and large start-ups, and strategy consulting. Owned and delivered on multi-billion dollar P&Ls and projects. Built a $1.5B start-up from the ground up. Worked across Europe (11 years) and Asia (19 years), from developing strategies to scaling, running, and optimizing complex businesses. Deep experience across enterprise technology, SaaS platforms, and digital commerce, developing and executing go-to-market strategies in complex, matrixed organizations. A team player and mentor who is thrilled to work with his teams across borders without hierarchies.

Current Focus:
Advising and investing in frontier AI firms including OpenAI, Anthropic, xAI, Perplexity, and Groq, while actively exploring the next senior operating leadership role. Featured AI commentator on CNBC, keynote speaker, and bylined author in the South China Morning Post, Nikkei Asian Review, and China Daily.

Previously:
• Global Chief Commercial Officer at MODIFI - a high growth fintech: Led a diverse, high-performing  commercial team across key global markets, focused on digital trade financing and cross-border B2B payments.
• Head of Apple’s multi-billion App Store Commerce and Services business in Asia Pacific: Member of the WW leadership team.
• Partner at tier-1 strategy consulting firms: Booz & Co, Gao Feng Advisory Company.
• Founder and brain father of several digital start-ups: Including a US$1.5B-valued unicorn.
• Corporate leadership roles: Luxury retail (LVMH), entertainment/media (Bertelsmann), and chemicals (BASF).

Master’s degree in Business Administration from the University of Bayreuth, Germany.
8074c3b108112ffb.top_skills: Business Strategy • Business Development • Sales • Leadership • Artificial Intelligence (AI)

My main area of expertise is Financial Engineering, Analytical Finance and Data Science. More information about my skills and projects I've worked on is available under "Projects".

3b00b5806cef3e61.headline: The Biggest Legend
3b00b5806cef3e61.about:
2022: Research Engineer at startup training large language models
2021: Research Engineer at FAIR training large language models + writing papers
2020: Research Engineer at HuggingFace working on distilling huge transformer models and improving sequence2sequence support (tasks like summarization, translation, dialogue) for the most popular open source NLP library in the whole wide world https://github.com/huggingface/transformers 

2019: Moved to SF. Worked on suggesting responses to doctors in doctor/patient chats at Curai https://arxiv.org/abs/1910.03476   and some research  on Data Augmentation Tricks for Text Classification https://bit.ly/2CGvXYl at Stanford.

2015-2018: My industry experience is evenly split between ML on timeseries, image and text data.  At Kensho, where I spent the last 4 years, I built and managed a 5 engineer team, but got to still spend most of my time running experiments and coding. Our product predicted demand for securities using proprietary transaction data and historical market data.  After Kensho got bought by S&P Global, I started working on entity linking from databases and free text, using ML.  I’ve also worked on computer vision on medical scans. 

At Merantix, a startup in Berlin, we used faster-rcnn to find tumors in breast scans. On Kaggle, I worked on finding lung occlusions in pneumonia patients using a combination of retinanet and mask-rcnn.  I also got a silver medal in a Kaggle competition that was more traditional Natural Language Understanding: finding duplicate Quora questions using bidirectional LSTMs.


Before Kensho, I graduated from Yale ('15), where my Economics thesis, which analyzed Peer to Peer lending, won the prize for the best undergraduate finance thesis. I've had internships with an education non-profit, two pro sports teams, and a hedge fund in Brazil.

Undergraduate Thesis (November '14- April '15): github.com/sshleifer/ProsperThesis

23c706eab4e36d94.headline: Founder & Managing Director at FSF Group | AI Strategy & Data Analysis | US–UK Market Specialist
23c706eab4e36d94.about:
Founder and Managing Director of FSF Group, a UK-based consultancy focused on AI, data analytics, and emerging technology strategy.

My work bridges US and UK technology ecosystems, drawing on hands-on experience gained during an extended professional period in the United States. While in the US, I developed a strong foundation in artificial intelligence and data-driven decision-making, working alongside leading practitioners and gaining exposure to cutting-edge AI research and deployment.

In April 2025, I worked part-time as a Data Analyst (Contract) with Thinking Machines Lab, supporting analytical and data-focused initiatives within an advanced research environment. This role strengthened my applied understanding of AI systems, data pipelines, and real-world model evaluation.

After returning to the UK in August 2025, I founded FSF Group to provide organisations with practical AI insight, strategic guidance, and access to transatlantic expertise. FSF Group supports clients seeking to understand, adopt, and operationalise AI responsibly and effectively.

My background also includes early-stage company formation, commercial operations, and strategic oversight, giving me a grounded, business-first approach to emerging technologies.

83108c95c4234730.headline: Member of Technical Staff @ Thinking Machines Lab | ex-Meta | Stanford | Physics x Scaling x AI
83108c95c4234730.about:
Senior Staff Engineer at Meta leading Monetization GenAI Foundation.

I specialize in algorithm-system co-design for revenue-critical GenAI products. My work bridges RLHF strategy with large-scale infrastructure to align generative content with ads performance data. I led post-training systems for multi-modality and optimize inference on 20k+ GPU fleets, leveraging my computational physics background to solve fundamental numerical stability challenges in highly efficient RL.

Stanford Physics M.S. | Peking University B.S.

45081f42373a4c8a.about: I am passionate about building general intelligent systems that process information at scale and assist humans in various knowledge-intensive tasks. For more of my research, please visit 🌐 https://victorialin.org

3db8c83a5e49c968.headline: AI Research Engineer
3db8c83a5e49c968.about: I am a machine learning engineer and researcher with a PhD in computer science. As one of the first engineers at Axiom, I drive research in machine learning for mathematics, focusing on scaling and advancing AI models. Previously at FAIR, I co-developed LLM Compiler and Code World Model. LLM Compiler is a family of foundation models trained on compiler intermediate representations and assembly code to automate and improve optimization tasks. Code World Model is Meta’s 32B open-weights code LLM mid-trained on execution traces and agent–environment trajectories to model program state and improve code generation and reasoning. My academic and industry work includes multiple peer-reviewed and distinguished publications on large language models, compiler optimization, and machine learning for systems.

8d00b2b222223a3b.headline: Member of Technical Staff @ Thinking Machines Lab
8d00b2b222223a3b.about:
I was the tech lead of research inference team in OpenAI. Before joining OAI, I was a software engineer at Facebook AI Infra, leading the effort of building inference system for recommendation and generative AI model. 

Previously, I received the Ph.D. degree in electrical engineering from Fudan University, Shanghai, China in 2010. After graduation, I worked as a postdoctoral researcher in EECS department, Northwestern University before I joined industry in Bay Area. 

Specialties: Software Engineering, Algorithms, Distributed Computing, System Level Optimization, Machine Learning 

Publication: http://scholar.google.com/citations?user=prBXsm8AAAAJ

77bebda5a3e6fa77.headline: Member of Technical Staff at Thinking Machines Lab
77bebda5a3e6fa77.about:
I am motivated by high-impact, meaningful problems in AI—particularly those that connect frontier research with real-world impact.

I am currently a Member of Technical Staff at Thinking Machines Lab. Previously, I founded and led the engineering and research development of AI Refinery at Accenture, building the company’s first enterprise AI agent platform. I scaled the organization from 3 to 80 research scientists and engineers, defined its technical vision and standards, coached tech leads, built hiring pipelines, and drove research innovation. The platform has contributed hundreds of millions in sales since its release.

I received my PhD in computer science from Massachusetts Institute of Technology, advised by Regina Barzilay. My research focused on transfer learning, interpretability, and fairness in machine learning, with an emphasis on building reliable and responsible AI systems.

Personal Website: https://www.yujia.io
Google Scholar: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=Ee4Peu4AAAAJ&hl=en
